name: 1-2-3-4 Cake by James Beard, Chef & Cooking Teacher
recipeIngredient: FOR THREE 9X1/2 INCH DEEP CAKE PANS
3 cups sifted cake flour (or all-purpose flour)
8 ounces unsalted butter, at room temperature (2 sticks)
recipeInstructions:
James Beard's 1-2-3-4 Layer Cake; Uses three 9x1/2 inch deep cake pans. Makes: One 3-layered cake; Serves 12 depending on thickness of slices.
Note: If you can't find cake flour, the same amount of all-purpose flour can be substituted.
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Using your hands, butter the bottom and sides of all three layer-cake pans with softened butter; sprinkle flour inside, then shake pans so you get a thin coating on the butter. Tip out any excess!
3. To sift flour: Lay a large piece of waxed paper on a board, put a dry measuring cup in the center, hold a sifter directly over it, scoop cake flour from the package into the sifter, and sift the flour directly into the cup. When the cup is full, draw the back of a knife blade lightly across the top of the cup (don't shake the flour down, or it will become dense) and then tip the measured flour into a mixing bowl. Repeat with the other 2 cups of flour.
4. Put the baking powder and salt in the sifter, holding it over the mixing bowl, and sift it over the flour. Then, still using your hands, mix the baking powder and salt lightly with the flour.
5. Put the butter into a second large mixing bowl. If it is very firm (it shouldn't be if left out of the fridge), squeeze it through fingers until softened. When soft enough to work, form right hand into a big fork and cream the butter by beating firmly and quickly with your hand, aerating it until light, creamy, and fluffy.
6. Gradually cream the 2 cups sugar into the butter using the same fork-like motion, beating until very light and fluffy. The mixture will lighten in color to almost white.
7. Wash and dry hands thoroughly. Separate eggs: let whites slip through fingers into a small bowl; drop yolks into a larger bowl. Beat yolks for a few minutes with a whisk until well blended. Then beat them thoroughly into the butter-sugar mixture.
8. Alternate beating milk and sifted flour (keep fingers close together) into the mixture, beating until batter is well mixed. Add vanilla and beat in for 1-2 minutes.
9. Beat egg whites with a large whisk or electric hand beater until they form soft, drooping peaks, then firm, glossy peaks. Do not overbeat to stiff and dry; tip onto cake batter and fold gently using your hand as a spatula, cutting down through the mixture and flipping it over.
10. Pour batter into prepared pans, dividing equally. Knock on countertop to level. Place in center of oven or stagger on middle racks if needed. Bake for 25 minutes.
11. Check doneness by lightly touching the center of each cake; it should spring back. Remove from oven, place on wire racks, loosen with a knife around sides, invert onto racks (top side down), then reverse layers so top side is up.
12. For filling and icing, see separate 1-2-3-4 Filling and Icing recipe.
